patch.value:
Nightly Validation windows-latest / Node 24 red since 2026-07-11 (GH-522 filed off the 2026-07-12 run 29182914361 on c7b56e73; the 07-11 leg failed identically but was masked by the macOS pm-dprb failure fixed in PR#520).

Bisect (clean): 07-10 nightly green on 1bdca537 (pre-PR#491); PR#491 (b5a41fba, merged 2026-07-10 10:25) introduced buildInitNextSteps pm-path scoping — every scoped hint renders through renderPmCommand → quoteCommandArg (src/cli/argv-utils.ts:41).

Root cause: quoteCommandArg's safe charset `[A-Za-z0-9._:/@=-]` omits backslash, and its escaper doubles `\`. Any native Windows tracker path (e.g. C:\tmp\tracker) therefore renders as `--pm-path "C:\\tmp\\tracker"`. This is a PRODUCT defect, not just a spec mismatch: the copy-paste hint is POSIX-shell quoting shown to cmd/PowerShell users — doubled backslashes are a literal wrong path there.

Failing specs (windows-only, deterministic): tests/unit/commands/init-command.spec.ts (covers init option normalizers / normalized prefix / preset registration / guidance status — all assert bare `${path.resolve(...)}` in hints) + tests/integration/init-path-guard.integration.spec.ts (path-like positional guard).

Fix direction (code-side, NOT spec-side — pm-gf6f precedent shows spec-only fixes let the class recur): platform-aware quoting — on win32 quote only when the arg needs it and never backslash-escape path separators (cmd/PowerShell rules); keep POSIX behavior (incl. the `$`-escaping the replacement-sensitive spec pins) unchanged. Alternative (admitting `\` to the safe charset) breaks paths with spaces and mis-escapes inside quotes — rejected.

Oracle gap: PR CI's windows-regression job does not run these suites, so nightly is the sole oracle for this class (same gap shape as pm-dprb/GH-510 on macOS).
GH-522: Windows nightly red — init next-steps hints POSIX-quote native Windows paths (quoteCommandArg backslash escaping)

quoteCommandArg (or a platform-aware wrapper) renders native Windows paths in scoped init hints copy-paste-valid for cmd/PowerShell (no doubled backslashes); the 5 failing specs (4x init-command.spec.ts, 1x init-path-guard.integration.spec.ts) pass on windows-latest Node 24 without weakening the POSIX replacement-sensitive quoting expectations; next Nightly Validation windows leg green

author: maintainer-agent

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Blast radius (2026-07-12 pass): renderPmCommand has 9 call sites across src/cli/{error-guidance,commander-usage,help-json-payload,main,commands/init}.ts — every rendered retry/recovery/usage hint that embeds a native Windows path (e.g. --pm-path values in suggested_retry bundles) ships the same doubled-backslash POSIX escaping, not just init next-steps. The nightly specs only pin the init surface; the fix must land centrally in quoteCommandArg so all surfaces inherit it. Do NOT fix per-call-site.

value.text: External-report sweep 2026-07-12: GitHub issue #522 remains the canonical open Windows nightly failure; no duplicate item created. This performance lane will not change its status or implementation scope.

Four init-command specs plus init-path-guard reproduced this canonical native-path quoting defect; macOS and Ubuntu Node 22/24/25 legs passed.
